name: dns_check
explanation: The DNS check is a synthetic probe: the user nominates a `hostname` (required) to resolve via `dns.resolver.Resolver().query()` and the check measures resolution time and optionally validates the result against an expected `resolves_as` IP list. There is no upstream service co-located with the Agent; the target is whichever name the user wants to monitor (e.g. `www.example.com`, an internal service name, or a CNAME being failover-tested). The required `hostname` is user *intent*, not a discoverable property. This is pattern K (synthetic / user-intent probe), with no credentials involved, but in this case the missing input is the target identity itself, so auto-config cannot synthesize a default instance.
notes: Pattern K (synthetic probe). Unlike http_check/tcp_check/tls (custom because they at least have a discoverable network endpoint to fingerprint), dns_check's target is purely a user-chosen hostname string with no on-host signal. Method `other` (no auth required, but not custom either since there is nothing to discover).
